characters.name: "Mad Dog" Morgendorffer
characters.description: "Mad Dog" MorgendorfferInformationGenderMaleAgeDeceasedOccupationArmyFamilyJake Morgendorffer(son)Ruth Morgendorffer(wife)Helen Morgendorffer(daughter-in-law)Daria Morgendorffer(granddaughter)Quinn Morgendorffer(granddaughter) "Mad Dog" Morgendorfferwas the deceased father ofJake Morgendorffer. His real name is unknown, only his grotesque nickname (a reference to the filmMad Dog Morganby the writers). While it wasn't stated outright on the show,Glenn Eichlersaid in a 2005 interviewhe was supposed to have been in the U.S. Army as part of the draft. He died in his early-to-mid forties (Jake of Hearts), after Jake and Helen married—a wedding he didn't bother to turn up to (Of Human Bonding).The Daria Huntermentions he wore contacts. While he never appears, his presence is felt in many episodes in the series, starting inThe Teachings of Don Jake, as Jake rants about the way his father treated him. It is heavily implied that Jake's father constantly berated him, being verbally abusive and never gave him any encouragement throughout his childhood.

characters.name: Adam
characters.description: AdamInformationGenderMaleResidentLawndale Adamis abackgrounderstudent inDaria'sclass atLawndale High. Adam is a tall student and one of the few black teenagers atLawndale High. He always wears a long light button-down jacket with rolled-up sleeves, usually amber in color, and has cornrows with beads tied to the ends. Usually when he is seen, Adam's quite calm and passive, though he was bored stiff duringMr. O'Neill'sclass inThe F Word. Like many students, he wore school colors forVal'svisit, and he had a blue version of his usual jacket. InI Loathe a Parade, he was seen in the company of his classmateJenna; they're likely friends or may have been dating at the time. He is mentioned inPierce Me, as someone who'd askedQuinnout on a date, and Quinn says that she hopes a caller was not Adam because she was going to cancel with Adam to go out with Simon.

characters.name: Amanda Lane
characters.description: Amanda LaneFirst appearanceArts 'N CrassLast appearanceArt BurnEpisode count3InformationGenderFemaleAge40s-50sOccupationPotterFamilyVincent Lane(Husband)Trent Lane(Son)Jane Lane(Daughter)Penny Lane(Daughter)Wind Lane(Son)Summer Lane(Daughter)Adrian Lane(Grandson)Courtney Lane(Granddaughter)ResidentLawndale Trent Lane(Son)Jane Lane(Daughter)Penny Lane(Daughter)Wind Lane(Son)Summer Lane(Daughter)Adrian Lane(Grandson)Courtney Lane(Granddaughter) Amanda Laneis the matriarch of theLane Familyand the wife ofVincent Lane. She is also the mother ofJane,Trent,Penny,Wind, andSummer. Amanda Lane'smore carefree parenting style consists mainly of leaving her children alone for long stretches of time with little or no contact, preferring to let them discover their own way in the world. She has a motto of "If you tried to hold a butterfly tightly in your hand, it will die. You have to let it go and if it comes back, it is truly yours. But if it wasn't, it never really was." When she is around, she can normally be found in the basement making pottery.

characters.name: Amelia
characters.description: AmeliaFirst appearanceCamp FearLast appearanceCamp FearVoiced bySarah DrewEpisode count1InformationGenderFemale Ameliais a former camper atCamp GrizzlyandDaria Morgendorffer'sonly known fan/follower at camp. She is voiced bySarah Drew, the voice ofStacy. Amelia, full view Amelia is a friendly and cheerful girl who is somewhat introverted. At the end ofCamp Fearshe gains the courage; she stands up toSkip Stevens. She saw herself and Daria as having a lot in common. In fact, the main reason Amelia came to the reunion was so that she could meet Daria again. She idolized Daria as the ultimate rebel, though many of Daria's "rebellious" acts during her stay at Camp Grizzly could be explained through either bad luck or Daria being ostracized. She's stunned when Daria reveals the truth behind some events - she didn't boycott the End-of-Summer campfire but wasn't invited - but in other cases, like Daria's horse running off and tossing her, Ameliahadknown the truth at the time but somehow remembered it as Daria being cool. Amelia's adoration of Daria is so great that when Jane refers to Amelia as "Daria having a secret fan club," Amelia takes the statement literally and assumes that Daria has anactualfan club. In Amelia's imagination, both she and Daria are examples of independent people "not following the crowd"... or at least she wanted tobean independent person that didn't follow the crowd. She later admits that she always did what people like Skip Stevens told her to do, whether she wanted to do it or not, out of fear of social rejection.

characters.name: Andrea
characters.description: AndreaFirst appearanceEsteemstersLast appearanceIs It College Yet?Voiced bySusie Lewis-Lynn(seasons 1-3)Lisa Collins(seasons 4-5)Episode count10InformationGenderFemaleAge16-18OccupationHigh school student; stock clerk atPaydaySignifcant Other(s)Charles Ruttheimer(Is It College Yet?)ResidentLawndale Lisa Collins(seasons 4-5) AndreaisDaria'sgoth classmate atLawndale High. She was first seen in the very first episode ofDaria,Esteemsters, as a member ofMr. DeMartino'shistory class. The mystery goth was an unnamed background character until the fourthDariaepisode of season one,Cafe Disaffecto. Unlike many other characters inDaria, virtually nothing is revealed about Andrea during the five years thatDariaran on MTV. The tone is set in "Cafe Disaffectowith the end of Andrea's coffeehouse poem, which concludes "I'm here. But where are you? Sure, I see your body. Anybody home in that rotting bag of flesh?" The few hints throughout the series regarding Andrea confirm her cynical outlook on life. Her locker is adorned with a skull and crossbones poster and a biohazard poster (Arts 'N Crass). She is happy to make a bet on Mr. DeMartino getting injured in the DJ/Faculty roller hockey game (and it is implied that she wins the betting pool). When Quinn foolishly tries to raise money from Lawndale High Students so she can get plastic surgery (Too Cute), Andrea asks Quinn if she's worried there might be a hell. Andrea's parents make her work at Payday (Mart of Darkness), and she's not happy about it. In (Daria's Inferno), Daria gives Andrea a cowbell to put in her nose to annoy her parents, and Andrea says: "Thatisirritating! Perfect." Daria's Inferno is Daria's dream, but if this is an indication of how seriously Daria takes Andrea and her goth sub-culture, it could be a reason why the two aren't friends or even acquaintances, even though they seem to have a lot in common. The only other hint of Andrea's inner life comes from cartoon strips inThe Daria Databasedrawn by "Andrea 666" calledGothic Nights: The Adventures of Queen Hecuba.The three cartoon strips deal with Queen Hecuba (a thinner, more regal version of Andrea) vanquishing enemies consisting of Upchuck, Kevin, the Lawndale High School faculty (except Janet Barch) and Mack Mackenzie. While the reasons for her antipathy toward the others is understandable, her beef with Mack is puzzling—the other enemies are all tricked into defeat and humiliations, while "Sir Mack the Fearless" is Hecuba's "archenemy" and requires fighting. That strip ends with a spectral version of Barch arriving, which terrifies Mack into retreat and causes Hecuba to give Barch a job; this makes Barch the only teacher given a positive role in the strip. Does Andrea like Barch? In one strip, Queen Hecuba seeks to rescue her "beloved brother Damien" from an evil king. It is unclear if the unseen Damien is based on a real-life brother of Andrea's. Daria dreams of Andrea working for Upchuck and massaging his feet in "Murder, She Snored."W

characters.name: Andrew Landon
characters.description: Andrew LandonFirst appearanceGiftedLast appearanceIs It College Yet?Voiced byBart FasbenderEpisode count5InformationGenderMaleAge50OccupationBuisnessmanFamilyMichele Landon(wife)Jodie Landon(daughter)Rachel Landon(daughter)Evan Landon(son)ResidentLawndale Andrew Landonis the husband ofMichele Landonand the father ofJodie Landon. He is often referred to as "The Folding Coffee Cup Guy". He is voiced byBart Fasbender. Andrew seen withTom,Daria, &Jodie Andrew seems to be perpetually looking for business connections and wanting to know who's who in the business world. He's encouragedJodie(andDaria) to follow his example and do the same thing. When talking about the institutionalized racism and sexism at Wizard Computers, he asked them "Do you walk away because the guy at the top is an idiot, or do you join the people trying to change the way he does business?" Andrew also encouraged Jodie to go to Crestmore because of the important contacts she'd make. Socially, he always seems cheerful and positive, shaking hands and being personable to everyone he meets, even if he's putting them down. He's been extremely friendly toJake Morgendorfferboth times they've met (Gifted,Of Human Bonding), even though the end ofGiftedmade it clear the Landons thought the Morgendorffers were bizarre people. InBondinghe made a backhanded comment about the size of Jake's business compared his own, stating the pickings were too small forhimbut Jake should be able to get something; he seems to have intended this as encouragement. Politically,GiftedandIs It College Yet?show him to be quite right-wing. He's virulently against welfare (he claims "wlfare cheats" force companies to use foreign child labor) and is using "creative accounting" on his tax returns to avoid subsidizing it. He may have some ties to Congressman Sachs since Jodie got an internship at his office (although the show does not state the political affiliation of the Congressman). He's aware of (and contemptuous of) the bigotry at Wizard Computers but points to the Wizard Foundation scholarship as evidence that some of its employees want to change that, and believed that this faction could win out if girls like Jodie and Daria would sign up. Jodie has remarked that he "thinks he's Martin Luther King" when he's trying to give a pep talk, and can say some ridiculous things in the attempt. He is somewhat overbearing and can dominate the conversation, and make some very bad, embarrassing jokes, such as in "Is It Fall Yet?" when he made a lame one in front ofTomabout his mother's "power" at Winged Tree Country Club. He appears to not realize when he's done this.

Jodie has remarked that he "thinks he's Martin Luther King" when he's trying to give a pep talk, and can say some ridiculous things in the attempt. He is somewhat overbearing and can dominate the conversation, and make some very bad, embarrassing jokes, such as in "Is It Fall Yet?" when he made a lame one in front ofTomabout his mother's "power" at Winged Tree Country Club. He appears to not realize when he's done this.
characters.personality_section: Andrew seems to be perpetually looking for business connections and wanting to know who's who in the business world. He's encouragedJodie(andDaria) to follow his example and do the same thing. When talking about the institutionalized racism and sexism at Wizard Computers, he asked them "Do you walk away because the guy at the top is an idiot, or do you join the people trying to change the way he does business?" Andrew also encouraged Jodie to go to Crestmore because of the important contacts she'd make. Socially, he always seems cheerful and positive, shaking hands and being personable to everyone he meets, even if he's putting them down. He's been extremely friendly toJake Morgendorfferboth times they've met (Gifted,Of Human Bonding), even though the end ofGiftedmade it clear the Landons thought the Morgendorffers were bizarre people. InBondinghe made a backhanded comment about the size of Jake's business compared his own, stating the pickings were too small forhimbut Jake should be able to get something; he seems to have intended this as encouragement. Politically,GiftedandIs It College Yet?show him to be quite right-wing. He's virulently against welfare (he claims "wlfare cheats" force companies to use foreign child labor) and is using "creative accounting" on his tax returns to avoid subsidizing it. characters.name: Angela Li
characters.description: Angela LiFirst appearanceEsteemstersLast appearanceIs It College Yet?Voiced byNora LaudaniEpisode count29InformationGenderFemaleAge40s-50sOccupationPrincipal ofLawndale HighResidentLawndale Angela Liis a recurring character inDaria. She is the principal ofLawndale High. She is voiced byNora Laudani. A highly self-centered, paranoid, and scheming dictator, Ms. Li is the closest thing the show has to a villain. She often puts the interest and financial needs of the school before her actual students and teachers, usually to sponsor security measures like drug-sniffing dogs and electric fences. She once braved a blizzard to findMr. O'Neill, which Jane remarked as heroic until Daria pointed out it was to save the expensive film equipment. characters.name: Angie
characters.description: AngieFirst appearanceDaria Dance PartyLast appearanceIs It College Yet?Episode count7InformationGenderFemaleAge16-18OccupationStudent atLawndale HighCheerleaderResidentLawndale Cheerleader Angieis a student at Lawndale High School. She is a member of theLawndale High Cheerleading Squad. Angie is most commonly seen with the rest of theLawndale High Cheerleading Squad, and appears to be one ofBrittany'sclosest friends. Of all of the female characters inDaria, Angie might be the one most friendly with Brittany, a rare case of a main cast member having a strong friendship with a background character. InFat Like Me, Jane hypothetically asks Brittany what she would do if Angie cheated on her with Kevin. Brittany responds that Angie is her friend and that she would never do that. Angie's role appears to be the breaker of bad news to Brittany. It is Angie's job to inform Brittany that her date, Robert, dances like a "geek." She tells Brittany (off-screen) that Kevin was "smiling (his) head off" at a lady customer purchasing nuts inIt Happened One Nut, which provokes an angry response and leads to Brittany confronting Kevin at the nut stand. She is first seen inDaria Dance Party, as one of several cheerleaders who are friends with Brittany Taylor. DuringI Loathe a Parade, Angie is on a float with Kevin. She is wearing a sash marked "FOOTBALL SWEETHEART." When Kevin tries to come on to her, Angie replies that she's unhappy with how Kevin is acting and that Brittany, her friend and Kevin's girlfriend, is right behind them. When Brittany attempts to become unpopular for a class assignment inThe F Word, Angie seems the most concerned about her, commenting that she's "lost her way" and "doesn't know who she is", and is the only one to argue that Lisa shouldn't be trying to replace the girl. Nevertheless, she reluctantly goes along with Lisa in (temporarily) kicking Brittany off the team and having Jane try out as her replacement. Brittany is reconciled with her fellow cheerleaders—and Angie—at the end of the episode. Angie, along with the other cheerleaders, gets into Great Prairie State University inIs It College Yet?She notes that that was theonlyschool that any of them got accepted to.

characters.name: Anthony DeMartino
characters.description: Anthony DeMartinoFirst appearanceEsteemstersLast appearanceIs It College Yet?Voiced byMarc ThompsonEpisode count26InformationGenderMaleAge40s-50sOccupationHistory TeacherUnion LeaderResidentLawndale Union Leader  DeMartino has a trademark habit of shouting every fourth or fifth word that he speaks, whereupon his right eye would bulge dangerously. According toThe Daria Diaries, he was forty-eight at the start of the show (and presumably was fifty-one at series conclusion). He also served in the Vietnam War, thus explaining his hyper-anger management issues.

characters.name: Arno
characters.description: ArnoFirst appearanceOf Human BondingLast appearanceOf Human BondingEpisode count1InformationGenderMaleResidentLawndale Arnois a Danish balloon crew chief employed byTerry Perry Barlowwho appears in the episodeOf Human Bonding. Arno is a tall and very muscular man. He has short cut blond hair with tan skin, a somewhat large nose and thin eyebrows. He also has a gap in his teeth from when he once saved a balloon crew by opening a rip panel with his teeth. He wears a brown sweatshirt with slightly lighter brown khaki pants and black laced boots that are not dissimilar toDaria's. While Terry claims to have once saved a balloon crew by opening a rip panel with his teeth, it was actually Arno who did so and he threatens to expose the story, claiming he has the dental damage to prove it. Terry later asked Arno to help him out with taking Jake and Daria on an early morning balloon run during the events ofOf Human Bonding. Arno was quite bemused when Terry not only wanted to go ballooning in bad weather, but didn't think he needed a proper balloon crew but just "a teenager and a confused man?" The resulting argument, with both him and Terry threatening each other, caused him to bemoan how he missed the chance to be the first to solo balloon around the world to work for Terry, and that feels that he has failed his native Denmark. When Daria jokes he could be the first to solo around the world twice, Arno takes her seriously and immediately leaves to do so and is not seen or mentioned again after that.

characters.name: Artie
characters.description: ArtieFirst appearanceEsteemstersLast appearanceA Tree Grows in LawndaleEpisode count3InformationGenderMaleAgeLate teens-early twentiesOccupationPizza Delivery BoyResidentLawndale Artieis a red-haired UFO buff and pizza delivery boy (later aPizza Kingwaiter) who first appeared inEsteemstersand later inThe Lawndale Fileand also inA Tree Grows in Lawndale.  He was also an obstacle of sorts in the Daria video gameDaria's Inferno. Artie in the series is portrayed as an obsessive nearly-psychotic, barely functioning UFO nut. In his second appearance, it's made clear his obsession prevents him from holding down a job: he's been repeatedly fired, something he blames on the aliens but is more because he does things likespend hourswhen on-the-job telling people about UFOs.  It is sometimes assumed that he is a student atLawndale High, though inA Tree Grows in Lawndaleit's revealed he isn't.

characters.name: Ashley-Amber Taylor
characters.description: Ashley-Amber TaylorFirst appearanceThe Old and the BeautifulLast appearanceGroped by an AngelEpisode count2InformationGenderFemaleAge26OccupationPoster Model for St. Peter Girl beer (former)FamilySteve Taylor(husband)Brittany Taylor(stepdaughter)Brian Taylor(stepson)Signifcant Other(s)Steve Taylor(husband)ResidentLawndale Brittany Taylor(stepdaughter)Brian Taylor(stepson) Ashley-Amber Tayloris a member of the Taylor family, the current wife ofSteve Taylorand the stepmother ofBrittany TaylorandBrian Taylor. FromThe Daria Database, it is shown that Ashley-Amber is twenty-six-years-old at the start of the series. She metSteve Taylorat a photo shoot. Before she was married to Steve, she was the poster model for St. Peter Girl beer. Her slogan was: "I'll cure what 'ales' you." In her speaking parts, Ashley-Amber seems to be quite dim and has a strong big-sister relationship with Brittany, who enjoys hanging out with her. InThe Old and the Beautiful, her first speaking role,Dariamistakes Ashley-Amber for Brittany's older sister when she answers the door. Due to her ditsy-ness, Ashley-Amber gets confused and thinks thereis possiblyan unknown Taylor she's never heard of. She then suggests she and this supposed Taylor sister could get manicures with her. She later appears inGroped by an Angelwhere she is first seen taking Brittany to the mall to buy glitter for Brittany's C-Minus party. When she asks a sales associate how glitter is made, she believes a lie he tells about it being made of crumpled up beams of sunlight. After she suggests that she and Brittany can save money by doing that themselves, the associate asks her if she's seeing anyone. Later at Brittany's party,Upchuckhits on her unaware of who she is. After she is finally able to get it out that she's Brittany's stepmother, Upchuck is at first shocked, but decides to go for it anyway. After he realizes that she has walked away, he starts wandering through the party trying to find her again calling out her name. The Daria Database(written before her first speaking appearance) adds an unexpected new depth to her, stating that without Steve realizing she's "secretly learning the joint-property laws". It also states Brittany uses Ashley-Amber's ID to get into clubs.

characters.name: Axl
characters.description: AxlFirst appearancePierce MeLast appearancePierce MeEpisode count1InformationGenderMaleOccupationOwner/operator ofAxl's Piercing Parlor Axlis the owner/operator ofAxl's Piercing Parlor, a body-piercing and tattoo shop inLawndale. Axl, full view Axl has a scruffy appearance, speaks with a lower class British cockney accent, and has a casual regard for hygiene and for the law - in fact, based on his own guide to keeping tongue-piercings clean inThe Daria Database, he knows bog all about hygiene. (According to that guide, health insurance companies refuse to cover you if you get injured or ill from using his services). However, inDaria's Inferno, he speaks with an American accent. He is said to have an unnamed apprentice who helps him run the counter while he is occupied with a job, but that apprentice is not there everyday so if someone else comes in during those cases he has to stop what he is doing to see what they want. He has a good relation withTrentandMoniquewho appear to be regular customers. InPierce Me, Trent takesDariato his shop after suggesting that she get a piercing while she is helping him shop for a birthday present forJane. He lets them know of a two for one special he is running, and shoots down Daria's suggestion of piercing her ears as the deal is for one hole each for two people, not two holes in one person. He suggests that she would look nice with a lip ring, then bends to Trent's suggestion that she get a naval ring. Before he can pierce her,Moniquecomes in and he gives her some antiseptic stuff for her nose ring. He encounters some difficulties piercing Daria due to her fear of pain, but he gets the job done after Trent holds her hand. He then tells her not to remove the ring for at least six months or it will close up and he'll have to pierce it again. He also tells her how to care for it and not to be alarmed if mucus starts pouring out. After Trent hears that Daria's piercing closed up by accident, Trent says that the next time they go there they'll get her a tattoo as those last forever.

characters.name: Axl
characters.description: AxlFirst appearancePierce MeLast appearancePierce MeEpisode count1InformationGenderMaleOccupationOwner/operator ofAxl's Piercing Parlor Axlis the owner/operator ofAxl's Piercing Parlor, a body-piercing and tattoo shop inLawndale. Axl, full view Axl has a scruffy appearance, speaks with a lower class British cockney accent, and has a casual regard for hygiene and for the law - in fact, based on his own guide to keeping tongue-piercings clean inThe Daria Database, he knows bog all about hygiene. (According to that guide, health insurance companies refuse to cover you if you get injured or ill from using his services). However, inDaria's Inferno, he speaks with an American accent. He is said to have an unnamed apprentice who helps him run the counter while he is occupied with a job, but that apprentice is not there everyday so if someone else comes in during those cases he has to stop what he is doing to see what they want. He has a good relation withTrentandMoniquewho appear to be regular customers. He suggests that she would look nice with a lip ring, then bends to Trent's suggestion that she get a naval ring. Before he can pierce her,Moniquecomes in and he gives her some antiseptic stuff for her nose ring. He encounters some difficulties piercing Daria due to her fear of pain, but he gets the job done after Trent holds her hand. He then tells her not to remove the ring for at least six months or it will close up and he'll have to pierce it again. He also tells her how to care for it and not to be alarmed if mucus starts pouring out. After Trent hears that Daria's piercing closed up by accident, Trent says that the next time they go there they'll get her a tattoo as those last forever.

characters.name: Bob
characters.description: BobFirst appearanceEsteemstersLast appearanceIs It College Yet?InformationGenderMaleAge16-18OccupationStudent atLawndale HighResidentLawndale Bobis the name of abackgroundergiven a name inCafe Disaffectoby happy accident after fans found a seating chart was legible. He's in a number ofDaria'sclasses. Bobis a teenage male with spiky teal hair. He wears a nose piercing connected to his ear. He wears a lavender muscle tank with ripped sleeves, khaki cargo shorts that are ripped at the knees, and dark brown boots. Bob is a standard punk and inJane's Addition, he is seen at the Zon smoking and drinking despite being too young. The obnoxious DJ's ofMental in the Morningmet with his visible approval inJake of Hearts. Bob even crashedBrittany'sparty inThe Invitation, driving a car at the time and likely without a license. This makes it clear as to why he was put inO'Neill'sself-esteem class inEsteemsters. Despite all this wild behavior outside of school, he repeatedly shows up at schoolbehaving: he's on the yearbook inThe New Kid, dons a tux inDaria Dance Party, supports a homecoming parade inI Loathe a Parade, and even helpsMr. DeMartinopack up the classroom inIs It Fall Yet?and got invited to Britany's party inGroped by an Angel.Most at odds with his image and bad habits, he's one of the guys who buy theFashion Club'snewsletter inThe Story of D(but doesn't actually read it). Aside from his fellow party crasher in his first appearance, Bob has only ever been seen hanging withBurnout Girland fellow punk-esque studentAngel.

characters.name: Bobby Stuart
characters.description: Bobby StuartFirst appearanceFire!Last appearanceFire!Episode count1InformationGenderMaleOccupationBellboy at Le Grand HotelResidentLawndale Bobby Stuartis a bellboy at Le Grand Hotel where the Morgendorffer's stayed inFire!. Bobby is a young man of average build with medium length light brown hair and blue eyes. He is shown wearing a standard red bellboy's uniform and hat. AfterJakenearly burned the Morgendorffer house down, they were forced to spend a few nights atLe Grand Hotelwhere Bobby worked as a bellboy. He became fixated onQuinnand promised that he could get her anything, claiming that his uncle owned the hotel. He began giving her free food and gifts, and eventually upgraded her to the Presidential Suite, which the Morgendorffer's insurance wouldn't cover, claiming he'd fixed it with his uncle as no one would be using it for awhile. He also asked her out to dates at C'est La Veal. His constant favors and attention caused a jealousSandito convince Quinn he was a stalker. Surprisingly, Bobby reallywasa stalker: the police investigated and informed Quinn that Bobby Stuart had no uncle: he'd been billing the Morgendorffers for everything, then hacked into the hotel's computer to delete the charges. A horrified Quinn focused on the important thing: You mean—I—almost—went out with—a computer geek?!" No word was given as to what happened to him after that. InLove is a Way Too Many Splendored Thing, Quinn warned: "Just goes to show, beware of geeks bearing gifts."

Brad makes his only appearance inDaria Dance Party. He and his brotherBrettgoes to Cumberland High but attend theLawndale Highdance portrayed in the episode, where they try to wooDariaandJane, Brad being the one to open negotiations. characters.description: Bret StrandFirst appearanceFair EnoughLast appearanceFair EnoughEpisode count1InformationGenderMaleAge16-18OccupationStudent atLawndale HighSignifcant Other(s)Stacy Rowe(former date)ResidentLawndale Bret Strandis a student atLawndale Highwho had a single date withStacyduring the events ofFair Enough.  Although he is not seen on-screen, he is mentioned as part ofStacy Rowe'ssubplot inFair Enough. Bret asked Stacy out and said he'd call her after the first date. He then never called her and pretended he hadn't seen Stacy when she approached him at the school's fair, so he can't have enjoyed the experience. His rejection caused Stacy to cry throughout the entire fair, much to the annoyance of everyone she encountered. It can be assumed he's popular: Stacy was excited to be asked out by him andTiffanywas interested as well.
